svg.title: The auth architecture: foundation, session & trust pillars, and what it defers by design
svg.desc: The authentication architecture in three groups. The foundation, in teal: one decision core that every login routes through, and a Critical and High hardening set under 832 tests. The session and trust pillars, in purple: converging admin login onto the core, refresh-token reuse-detection with family revocation, the server-side revocable session store via a custom ITicketStore and a dbo.UserSession table, the NIST 800-63B password model, step-up MFA on the money paths, and the JWKS ES256 issuer boundary — the trust pivot. Deferred by design, in muted dashed, each adopted only on a real trigger: Redis as an L2 cache when scaling to multiple web instances, a dedicated OpenIddict IdP when SSO or B2B federation arrives, a Duende BFF only if the web becomes a browser-JS SPA, and DPoP once tokens cross real trust boundaries. Hover or focus any item to read it.

g.text: foundation · session & trust pillars · deferred by design

g.aria-label: Foundation: one decision core that every login routes through, which fixed the already-logged-in bug by construction
g.title: one decision core · every login routes through it

g.aria-label: Foundation: the Critical and High hardening set, 2 Criticals plus 6 Highs plus 12 review findings, under 832 tests
g.title: Critical/High hardening set · 832 tests

g.aria-label: Pillar: converge the admin login onto the same core
g.title: admin login → the same core

g.aria-label: Pillar: refresh-token reuse-detection with family revocation
g.title: refresh reuse-detection + family revocation
g.text: FamilyId · a reused token kills the family

g.aria-label: Pillar: the server-side revocable session store via a custom ITicketStore and a dbo.UserSession table
g.title: revocable session store · ITicketStore + dbo.UserSession
g.text: ITicketStore + dbo.UserSession · delete a row = revoke

g.aria-label: Pillar: the NIST 800-63B Rev 4 password model with breached-password screening
g.title: NIST 800-63B password model

g.aria-label: Pillar: step-up MFA on the money and account-change paths
g.title: step-up MFA on money paths

g.aria-label: Pillar: the JWKS ES256 issuer boundary — the trust pivot that makes a drop-in OIDC server a config change
g.title: JWKS ES256 · the trust pivot
g.text: a drop-in OIDC server becomes a config change

g.aria-label: Deferred by design: Redis as an L2 cache, adopted when scaling to multiple web instances — a config-only flip
g.title: Redis L2 · on multi-instance scale (config-only flip)

g.aria-label: Deferred by design: a dedicated OpenIddict IdP, adopted when SSO or B2B federation arrives — rides on the existing user store
g.title: OpenIddict IdP · on SSO / B2B federation
g.text: same AspNetUsers store · the JWKS seam is the prereq

g.aria-label: Deferred by design: a Duende BFF, adopted only if the web app becomes a browser-JS SPA or Blazor-WASM front end
g.title: Duende.BFF · only if web becomes a browser-JS SPA

g.aria-label: Deferred by design: DPoP, adopted once tokens cross real trust boundaries like third-party clients or split services
g.title: DPoP · once tokens cross real trust boundaries

g.aria-label: The defining property: revocable, enumerable sessions on both edges — the web session store and the mobile refresh-family revocation together, unified into one Active Sessions and Devices screen
g.title: revocable, enumerable sessions on both edges
g.text: revocable, enumerable sessions on both edges
the web session store + mobile refresh-family revocation → one “Active Sessions & Devices” screen
the property that separates a good auth system from a top-1% one
two accepted ADRs after a 4-stream R&D review
